channel.title: briefUpdatesHealthOffshootNL
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesHealthOffshootNL, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: How to hone your 'friendship intuition'
item.description: Friendship expert Kat Vellos shares tips on how to make a new friendship stick, including what to do together, how often to hang out — and what to do ...

item.title: 'Part of something that's bigger than you': GoodGym joins volunteering and exercise
item.description: GoodGym, based in the UK, combines exercising with volunteering. How does this combination positively impact people?

item.title: Louisville Found PFAS in Drinking Water. The Trump Administration Wouldn’t Require Any Action.
item.description: After detecting a sudden spike in PFAS in its drinking water, the city traced it upstream along the Ohio River to a factory in West Virginia. But the ...

item.title: Alabama’s ‘Pretty Cool’ Plan for Robots in Maternity Care Sparks Debate
item.description: Alabama, a state with one of the nation’s highest infant mortality rates, is betting on robots to help fix its maternal care crisis. But the state’s p...

item.title: New Medicaid Work Rules Likely To Hit Middle-Aged Adults Hard
item.description: Republicans have said new rules requiring many Medicaid participants to work 80 hours a month will pinpoint unemployed young people who should have jo...

item.title: 2026: A Year for Reimagining Healthcare’s Safety Net
item.description: By JEFF BRANDES To say healthcare’s safety net experienced tectonic shifts in 2025 would be an understatement. The rapid introduction of multiple and ...

item.title: End of Enhanced Obamacare Subsidies Puts Tribal Health Lifeline at Risk
item.description: Tribal insurance programs give Native Americans access to affordable health care when the Indian Health Service falls short. Those plans are threatene...

item.title: Trust No One
item.description: By KIM BELLARD You know, it’s gotten to the point when I just try to tune out the things Robert F. Kennedy Jr. says. “Schizophrenia can be cured with ...

item.title: In a world built for sitting, here's how to stay active — even when stuck inside
item.description: In the office, classroom and living room, working and relaxing mean sitting still. Our bodies evolved without chairs. Here are some tips for getting o...

item.title: Hospitals are posting prices for patients. It's mostly industry using the data
item.description: The Trump administration pushed for price transparency in health care. But instead of patients shopping for services, it's mostly health systems and i...

item.title: This complex brain network may explain many of Parkinson's stranger symptoms
item.description: Parkinson's disease appears to disrupt a brain network involved in everything from movement to memory.

item.title: Listen to the Latest ‘KFF Health News Minute’
item.description: The "KFF Health News Minute” brings original health care and health policy reporting from our newsroom to the airwaves each week.

item.title: Carta Healthcare – AI for Registry Creation
item.description: It’s not well known but there’s a lot of people in hospitals who spend a lot of time creating patient registries for quality programs, CMS reporting, ...

item.title: Will HHS Enhance or Stall the Promise of Artificial Intelligence for Healthcare?
item.description: By STEVEN ZECOLA In its Strategy for Artificial Intelligence (V.3), the Department of Health and Human Services (“HHS”) acknowledges that: “For too lo...

item.title: Arnold Ventures Part II “Structuring Information Felicitously”
item.description: By JEFF GOLDSMITH In the first part of our look at Arnold Ventures, we explored its business model and generous support of elite University health pol...
